title: triticum vulgare var. aristatum
intro: Triticum vulgare var. aristatum, also known as wild rye or bearded wheat, is a type of cereal grass that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic. The health benefits of wild rye include reducing blood sugar levels, improving digestive issues, and aiding in the relief of anxiety and stress. Wild rye can be used in herbal preparations such as tea, tinctures, and infusions, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
